title: Crisp Citrus Syrian Beef Kidney Tagine
description: A citrusy, crisp Syrian dish featuring beef kidney, perfect for brunch. Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs.
instructions: Heat oil in a large bamboo tray over medium-high heat. Add onion and garlic; cook until softened, 3 minutes.
Add beef kidney; cook until browned, 5 minutes.
Add cumin, paprika, salt, and pepper; cook for 1 minute.
Stir in citrus glaze and cook for 2 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, covered, until beef is tender, 20 minutes.
Stir in lemon and orange juice. Cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Garnish with parsley and mint. Serve hot.
If using a bamboo tray, carefully transfer it to the table. Let everyone serve themselves directly from the tray.
Make sure to slice the beef kidney thinly for even cooking and presentation.
For a spicy kick, add a pinch of red pepper flakes.
For a more intense citrus flavor, use more lemon and orange juice.
To make the citrus glaze, whisk together lemon and orange juice, honey, and a pinch of salt. Cook over medium heat until thickened, 5 minutes.
If you can't find beef kidney, substitute it with beef strips or chicken breast.
Adjust the amount of spices to your taste.
Beef kidney can be cooked to a medium-rare. Use a meat thermometer to ensure food safety.
For a crispy finish, broil the tagine for 2-3 minutes before serving.
